underlying: Who finishes 2nd in the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary (Aug 18, 2026 certified results)
current_value_source: Emerson College Polling April 2026; Florida Phoenix; The Hill
method: Donalds is the dominant favorite for FIRST (Polymarket 84-90%, Emerson 46%, Trump-endorsed, $22M raised). The 2ND-PLACE question is wide-open and depends crucially on whether Casey DeSantis files. Allocate probability mass for 2nd: anchor on April Emerson + Polymarket inference + the rule that the 2nd-place market sums to ~0.93 across the listed candidates (Jay Collins 0.19, Donalds 0.10, Fishback 0.50, Renner 0.05, DeSantis 0.10) which leaves ~7% for missing/other. The market's heavy weight on Fishback (0.50) reflects (a) his $22M-class fundraising in the GOP donor world, (b) his 9.5% baseline in some polls vs Collins 4.5%, (c) absence of DeSantis filing risk. My model: P(DeSantis files & places 2nd) = 0.18 (she has not declared but is rumored to); P(Fishback 2nd | DeSantis-no) = 0.45 (financial firepower, name recognition build-up by August); P(Collins 2nd | DeSantis-no) = 0.22 (LG incumbency, Tallahassee establishment); P(Renner 2nd | DeSantis-no) = 0.07; P(Donalds 2nd | DeSantis-no) = 0.04 (he is heavy 1st-place fav so 2nd means an upset by another candidate); P(other 2nd) = 0.04. Combined unconditional: Fishback 0.37, Collins 0.18, DeSantis 0.18, Donalds 0.03, Renner 0.06; residual ~0.18 to a non-listed candidate (e.g., a late entrant, Matt Gaetz, or another). Adjust to fit available strikes — only 5 candidates listed; remaining 0.18 redistributes proportionally if mutually-exclusive event resolves to one of the 5. Renormalized: Fishback 0.45, Collins 0.22, DeSantis 0.22, Donalds 0.04, Renner 0.07.
notes: VERY EARLY market — primary is 4 months out and the field is still forming. Casey DeSantis filing is the central uncertainty: if she enters, she likely consolidates anti-Donalds vote and takes 2nd; if she doesn't, the Collins/Fishback contest depends on which can convert money/establishment vs. populist-outsider lanes. Key risks: (1) Trump endorsement of any 2nd-tier candidate would massively shift weights. (2) Donalds collapses (low prob ~5%) and the question becomes who finishes 2nd to a different 1st. (3) New entrant from FL congressional delegation (e.g., Gaetz, Diaz-Balart). Market prices: Fishback 0.50 (slightly rich vs my 0.45), Collins 0.19 (close to my 0.22), DeSantis 0.10 (CHEAP vs my 0.22 — buy yes), Donalds 0.15 (expensive vs my 0.04 — sell yes), Renner 0.05 (fair). The Donalds 2nd market at 0.10-0.15 is HARD TO JUSTIFY since that requires both (a) Donalds losing 1st (already only ~10-15% per Polymarket) AND (b) staying in 2nd not 3rd — joint prob <5%. Likely is a rules nuance I'm missing (perhaps it pays out if Donalds withdraws and finishes 2nd by ballot order?). Conf=LOW because: 4-month forward, candidate field still forming, sparse polling, large undecided block (39%), DeSantis filing binary.
